title: Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXXIX.
content:
How I love the festive boy,
    Tripping through the dance of joy!
    How I love the mellow sage,
    Smiling through the veil of age!
    And whene'er this man of years
    In the dance of joy appears,
    Snows may o'er his head be flung,
    But his heart--his heart is young.
meaning:
Thomas Moore's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Odes Of Anacreon - Ode XXXIX."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
